response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with web, mobile, and some embedded exposure, but the role demands a Principal-level hardware/SoC performance architect with deep expertise in AI accelerators, deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD, and GPU architecture. The resume shows no SoC/IP modeling, no hardware-software co-design at the accelerator level, no DNN training/inference optimization work, and no GPU architecture experience. Educational background (BS CS) without the matching 8+ years of hardware/performance-architecture experience further misaligns with the role's requirements.
strengths: Long tenure as senior/staff engineer with broad systems exposure
Some low-level/C/C++ work at National Instruments and DLang low-latency services
Demonstrated TDD, CI/CD, and engineering rigor across multiple roles
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or hardware performance-architecture experience
No deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD, or GPU architecture background
Career trajectory is application/web/full-stack — not principal-level hardware IC

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No DNN / GPU / SIMD-MIMD performance optimization background
